title: Section (Pattern H hierarchical derive)
description: Generalized per-section JSON shape shared across substrate domains (foundations, accessibility, …) emitted by the section-dist derive engine. The Pattern H tree mirrors heading paths from the per-section files under <domain>/src/ (concatenated at derive time). Leaves carry blocks[]; branches (_index.json) carry children[]. Schema is permissive (additionalProperties:true) because the derive script may add new metadata fields without breaking the contract.

_schema_version.description: Schema version for the section JSON shape. Currently 1. Bumps require coordinated plugin-side reader updates.

id.description: Slash-separated path identifier for the section (matches heading slugs). Empty string for the root _index.

title.description: Human-readable section title, as authored in the MD heading.

path.description: Ordered slug segments from root to this section. Empty array for the root. Mirrors the directory layout under <domain>/dist/.

order.description: Sibling ordering hint (1-based) reflecting authored heading order in the source MD. Auto-assigned by derive. Only present on child-list entries; per-leaf files inherit ordering from their parent _index.json children[].

parent.description: Slug of the parent section, or null for the root _index.

anchors.description: Heading anchors for cross-document linking. Each level (h1..h4) carries the slug used in MD anchor links.

source.description: Provenance — points back to the authored source MD + (for leaves) the line range.

file.description: Path to the source MD (repo-relative). Per-section split: this is the src/ directory; authoring lives in the per-section files inside.

body.description: Section's prose body (markdown). Empty when the section is purely structural.
examples: Use the spacing scale for all margin and padding values.

blocks.description: Structured blocks extracted from the section (tables, code fences, lists). Leaves carry blocks[]; branches typically don't. Each block has a type discriminator.

type.description: Block kind. Common values: table, code, list, paragraph.

children.description: Child section index entries (id + title + order). Present only on _index.json files. Sibling files under the same dir provide full content per child.

_meta.description: Auto-generation marker. Authors must not hand-edit derived files; this object documents that.

examples.do_not_edit: Edit the per-section src/ files; CI regenerates this file.

a11y_refs.description: P8 transversal reference taxonomy: WCAG criterion slugs from accessibility/dist/a11y-index.json that this section reinforces or is governed by. Authored as optional YAML frontmatter on the per-section src file; the derive attaches refs to the file's top-level H2 node (file-scoped, Option A — sub-section precision is deferred). Reuses the components/categories ref shape so AI/plugin consumers can resolve uniformly across substrate domains. Symmetric with `motion_refs`.

ref.description: Accessibility requirement slug. Must exist in accessibility/dist/a11y-index.json.

note.description: Optional section-specific note layered on top of the generic a11y guidance.
examples: applies to all hit targets in this section

motion_refs.description: P8 transversal reference taxonomy: motion pattern slugs from foundations/dist/tokens/motion.json#patterns that this section reinforces or is governed by. Same authoring + attachment model as `a11y_refs` above. Reuses the components/categories ref shape.

ref.description: Motion pattern slug. Must exist in foundations/dist/tokens/motion.json#patterns[*].slug.

note.description: Optional section-specific note layered on top of the generic motion guidance.
examples: section uses only the linear easing band

wcag.description: Optional WCAG success-criterion numbers (e.g. "1.4.3") this section maps to. Only relevant to the accessibility domain; foundations sections omit it. Distinct from `a11y_refs` (which references criterion slugs in a11y-index.json) — `wcag` carries the raw dotted criterion numbers.
